# MCP in 2026: Connectivity, Progressive Discovery, and Skills over MCP

**Source**: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v3Fr2JR47KA](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v3Fr2JR47KA)
**Processed**: 2026-04-21
**Speaker**: Anthropic MCP maintainer (self-identifies as Python SDK author; likely David Soria Parra — unverified)
**Venue**: MCP community talk / conference
**Duration**: ~18–20 min (inferred; auto-transcript has no timestamps)
**Language**: en (auto-generated)

---

## Summary

Anthropic's MCP roadmap talk framing **2026 as the year of connectivity**. Three-part thesis: (1) 2024 was demos, 2025 was coding agents, 2026 is general agents that need connectivity to 5+ SaaS apps; (2) no single connectivity tool fits all — the best agents use **skills + MCP + CLIs/computer use** together, picking the right tool per job; (3) agent harnesses need **progressive discovery** (tool search / deferred schema loading) and **programmatic tool calling** (code mode) to stop exhausting the context window. Also previews: MCP applications (servers ship their own UI), stateless transport (June 2026), cross-app access SSO, server discovery at well-known URLs, SDK v2 (TypeScript + Python, based on fast MCP), and **skills over MCP** as a protocol extension.

Milestone: 110M monthly downloads of MCP SDKs, driven largely by third-party frameworks (OpenAI Agent SDK, Google ADK, LangChain, thousands of others) — roughly 2× faster adoption than React's equivalent milestone [unverified].

Structured distillation filed at: `00-AREA/6I-CYBORG/AI-FRONTIER-OPS/260421-6I-CYBORG-MCP_2026_CONNECTIVITY.md`
